title.en: Marine Fasteners Guide: Saltwater-Resistant Fasteners for Marine Environments
title.zh: 海洋紧固件指南：海洋环境耐盐紧固件

description.en: Marine fastener selection guide: materials, coatings, and applications for coastal and marine environments. Stainless steel and specialized coatings.
description.zh: 海洋紧固件选择指南：沿海和海洋环境的材料、涂层和应用。

keywords: marine fasteners, saltwater resistant, stainless steel 316, coastal construction, hot-dip galvanizing

body.en:
Marine environments present the most demanding conditions for fasteners. Salt water, humidity, UV radiation, and mechanical stress from wave action and wind combine to accelerate corrosion and mechanical failure.

Standard carbon steel fasteners fail rapidly in marine environments - sometimes within months. Successful marine applications require careful material selection and often enhanced installation practices.

African coastal development includes ports, harbors, coastal hotels and resorts, marine farms, and coastal infrastructure - all requiring appropriate fastener solutions.

TradeGo supplies marine-grade fasteners for African coastal and offshore applications.
body.zh: 海洋环境对紧固件的要求最严苛。盐水、湿度、紫外线辐射和波浪和风的机械应力共同加速腐蚀和机械故障。

body.en:
Marine fasteners require superior corrosion resistance:

A4 (316) Stainless Steel: The standard for marine applications. Contains 16% chromium, 10% nickel, and 2% molybdenum. The molybdenum addition dramatically improves chloride corrosion resistance. Suitable for splash zone and below.

A2 (304) Stainless Steel: Acceptable for protected indoor marine environments. Not suitable for direct salt water exposure or splash zones.

Super Duplex Stainless (2507): Superior to 316 for extreme environments. Higher chromium and molybdenum content. Used in offshore platforms and demanding applications.

Nitronic 50: High-strength stainless with excellent marine corrosion resistance. Used in critical applications requiring strength plus corrosion resistance.

Titanium: Excellent strength-to-weight ratio and marine corrosion resistance. High cost limits use to specialized applications.

For most African coastal applications, A4 (316) stainless provides the best balance of cost and performance. Super duplex or titanium for critical offshore applications.
body.zh: A4（316）不锈钢是海洋应用的标准，含16%铬、10%镍和2%钼。超双相不锈钢用于极端环境，钛用于需要强度和耐腐蚀性的关键应用。

body.en:
When stainless steel is not feasible, protective coatings extend carbon steel fastener life:

Hot-Dip Galvanizing (HDG): 40-60 micrometer zinc coating. Good for atmospheric marine exposure above water. Replicates at 15-25 years in mild marine environments.

Dacromet: 8-12 micrometer coating with excellent salt spray resistance (1000+ hours). Performs better than HDG in some marine applications.

Geomet: Environmentally-friendly coating with 500+ hour salt spray resistance. Good for inland coastal applications.

Epoxy Coating: excellent chemical and water resistance. Applied to threaded areas requires care to maintain thread function.

Stainless Steel Cladding: Carbon steel core with stainless exterior. Combines strength of carbon steel with corrosion resistance of stainless. Cost-effective for large structural fasteners.

For underwater or splash zone applications, stainless steel (A4/316) or super duplex is strongly recommended over coated carbon steel.
body.zh: 热镀锌（HDG）用于大气海洋暴露，达克罗美具有良好的耐盐雾性能，环氧涂层具有优异的耐化学性和耐水性，不锈钢包层结合碳钢强度和不锈钢耐腐蚀性。

body.en:
Marine fastener selection by application zone:

Above Water (Atmospheric Zone):
HDG or Dacromet coated carbon steel adequate for structures above splash zone. A4 stainless for appearance-critical applications.

Splash Zone (intermittent wetting):
A4 stainless minimum. HDG carbon steel may last 5-10 years but will eventually fail. Super duplex for critical applications.

Below Water (Immersed Zone):
A4 stainless for fresh water immersion. Super duplex or higher for salt water. Coated carbon steel not recommended.

Offshore Platforms:
Super duplex stainless or specialized alloys. Critical structural connections require documented material certification.

Marina and Harbor Structures:
A4 stainless for dock furniture, cleats, and hardware. HDG acceptable for structural connections above water.

Marine Electronics Enclosures:
Stainless steel or plastic fasteners to prevent galvanic corrosion with electronic equipment.
body.zh: 水上区域：热镀锌或达克罗涂覆碳钢足够。飞溅区：A4不锈钢最低。水中：A4不锈钢用于淡水浸没，超双相用于盐水。

body.en:
Marine fastener installation requires extra care:

Thread Lubrication: Use appropriate marine-grade lubricant during installation. Prevents galling of stainless threads and ensures accurate torque.

Torque Control: Use calibrated torque wrench. Over-tightening damages threads and can cause stress corrosion cracking in stainless.

Dissimilar Metals: Avoid galvanic corrosion by not mixing stainless and carbon steel fasteners in the same connection. When unavoidable, use绝缘 washers and bushings.

Pre-Loading: Marine structures experience dynamic loads. Ensure adequate preload to prevent joint loosening from vibration.

Regular Inspection: Marine fasteners should be inspected annually for signs of corrosion, loosening, or fatigue. Replace any questionable fasteners immediately.

Documentation: Keep records of fastener materials, coatings, and installation torque values for maintenance and compliance.
body.zh: 海洋紧固件安装需要额外注意：螺纹润滑、扭矩控制、避免异金属配合、预加载、定期检查、文档记录。

body.en:
Why do stainless fasteners gall in marine environments?

Stainless steel is prone to galling (thread seizure) when installed without lubrication or with excessive torque. Always use marine-grade lubricant during installation.

Can I use galvanized fasteners underwater?

Not recommended. HDG works above water but rapidly degrades in saltwater immersion. Use A4 stainless or super duplex for underwater applications.

What's the difference between 304 and 316 stainless for marine use?

316 contains molybdenum which significantly improves chloride corrosion resistance. Use 304 only for protected indoor marine environments. Always use 316 for outdoor marine exposure.

How often should marine fasteners be inspected?

Annual inspection is minimum. More frequent inspection (6 months) for critical structures and harsh environments. Inspect for corrosion, loosening, and fatigue cracks.

Can I paint over stainless fasteners?

Not necessary and not recommended. Paint won't adhere well to stainless and may hide corrosion problems. Leave stainless fasteners unpainted.

What's the lifespan of A4 stainless in salt water?

Properly installed A4 stainless can last 20+ years in marine environments. Lifespan depends on installation quality, loading conditions, and specific environmental factors.
body.zh:
不锈钢紧固件在海洋环境中为什么会发生磨损？

不锈钢在安装时没有润滑或扭矩过大容易发生磨损。始终使用海洋级润滑剂。

我可以在水下使用镀锌紧固件吗？

不推荐。热镀锌在水上有效但在盐水中迅速降解。

cta.zh: 联系TradeGo获取海洋紧固件解决方案。
